data.title: Majority of Solana to BNB Chain Traders Face Losses
data.description: According to PANews, a statement from curb.sol, who identifies as Solana's nCMO, reveals that 96% of traders who have bridged assets from Solana to the BNB Chain are currently experiencing losses. The post did not provide details on the sample source or the statistical methods used.

data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $198 million yesterday, marking the ninth consecutive day of net inflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $198 million yesterday (October 9th, EST). The Blackrock IBIT ETF saw the largest single-day net inflow yesterday, with a net inflow of $255 million, bringing IBIT's total net inflow to $65.19 billion. The next largest single-day net inflow was the Bitwise BITB ETF, with a net inflow of $6.58 million, bringing BITB's total net inflow to $2.52 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs was $164.79 billion, with a net asset value ratio (market capitalization relative to the total Bitcoin market capitalization) of 6.83%, and cumulative net inflows to $62.77 billion.

data.title: CZ: Giggle Academy has received $11 million worth of BNB donations
data.description: Binance founder CZ posted on the X platform that in less than 20 days, Giggle Academy has received $11 million worth of BNB donations to support the promotion of free education around the world.

data.title: Binance to Adjust Leverage and Margin Tiers for Multiple Contracts
data.description: According to an official announcement, Binance Futures will adjust the leverage and margin tiers of several USD-M perpetual contracts at 2:30 PM (Beijing Time) on October 11, 2025, and 2:30 PM (Beijing Time) on October 14, 2025. Affected contracts include COAIUSDT, TAKEUSDT, NAORISUSDT, A2ZUSDT, ARIAUSDT, TSTUSDT, GUSDT, PUNDIXUSDT, 1000CATUSDT, HYPERUSDT, PROMPTUSDT, and ZECUSDT. (This newsletter was generated with AI assistance.)

data.title: Bitcoin’s Rally Still Looks Intact, CryptoQuant Says: Here’s Why
data.description:
On-chain analytics firm CryptoQuant has explained how there aren’t any signs of a Bitcoin price peak yet, based on this indicator. Bitcoin Net Realized Profit/Loss Is Still At Moderate Levels In a new post on X, CryptoQuant has shared the latest trend in the Bitcoin Net Realized Profit/Loss. This indicator tells us about whether the Bitcoin investors are selling their coins at a net profit or loss. The metric works by going through the transaction history of each token being spent to see what price it was moved at before this
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/bitcoin-news/bitcoins-rally-intact-cryptoquant-says-heres-why/

data.title: Ethereum spot ETF saw a total net outflow of $8.54 million yesterday, ending its 8-day streak of net inflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $8.54 million yesterday (October 9th, EST). The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net inflow yesterday was the Blackrock ETF ETHA, with a net inflow of $39.29 million, bringing ETHA's historical net inflow to $14.57 billion. The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow yesterday was the Fidelity ETF FETH, with a net outflow of $30.26 million, bringing FETH's historical net inflow to $2.72 billion. As of press time, the Ethereum spot ETF's total net asset value (NAV) was $29.90 billion, with a 5.71% ETF NAV ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Ethereum's total market capitalization), and a cumulative net inflow of $15.08 billion.

data.title: This week’s mining data highlights: Bitcoin mining company IREN announced the completion of the pricing of $875 million convertible senior notes
data.description: Week 42 of 2025 (October 3-9): 1. According to cloverpool, the average computing power of the Bitcoin network was 1012 EH/s, with a maximum of 1117 EH/s and a minimum of 885 EH/s, a decrease of 4.57% from the average computing power of last week (1061 EH/s). 2. According to blockchain.com, the average price of Bitcoin was US$122,630, with a maximum of US$126,296 and a minimum of US$119,328, an increase of 8.98% from the average price of last week (US$112,523). 3. Mining news worth noting include: (1) Bitcoin mining company IREN announced the completion of the pricing of US$875 million convertible senior bonds with a coupon rate of 0.00% and due in 2031; (2) JPMorgan Chase: The total market value of 14 US-listed Bitcoin mining companies reached US$56 billion in September, a record high. Data partner: Cango Inc. (CANG), a Bitcoin mining company listed on the New York Stock Exchange.
